comment: This is generated by ESQL's AbstractFunctionTestCase. Do not edit it. See ../README.md for how to regenerate it.
name: tstep
description:
Creates groups of values - buckets - out of a `@timestamp` attribute using a fixed `step` width.

Unlike `TBUCKET`,
which aligns buckets to calendar boundaries, `TSTEP` always buckets at the fixed interval increments in UTC timezone.
Each `bucket` label is rendered as the upper boundary of the half-open interval `(timestamp - step, timestamp]`.

The `step` can be a time duration (e.g. `1 hour`) or a target bucket count (an integer).
When a count is given, the actual step is chosen as the finest supported granularity
(from 1 millisecond up to 1 day) that produces at most `count` buckets in the `[from, to]` range.
Explicit `from` and `to` bounds are required when a count is given.

In the one-argument form, provide a
`@timestamp` range
in the request query filter; that range's start anchors the grid.
In the three-argument form, supply explicit `from` and `to` bounds directly; these take precedence over any
request `@timestamp` filter. `TSTEP` cannot be used together with `TRANGE`.

params.name: step
params.description: Fixed bucket width in UTC, or target number of buckets to fill the `from`-`to` range. When an integer count is given, the actual step is chosen as the finest supported granularity (from 1 millisecond up to 1 day) that produces at most `count` buckets in the `[from, to]` range. Explicit `from` and `to` bounds are required when a count is given.

params.name: from
params.description: Start of the time range that anchors the step grid. Required together with `to`.

params.name: to
params.description: End of the time range. Required together with `from`.

params.name: step
params.description: Fixed bucket width in UTC, or target number of buckets to fill the `from`-`to` range. When an integer count is given, the actual step is chosen as the finest supported granularity (from 1 millisecond up to 1 day) that produces at most `count` buckets in the `[from, to]` range. Explicit `from` and `to` bounds are required when a count is given.

params.description: End of the time range. Required together with `from`.

params.name: step
params.description: Fixed bucket width in UTC, or target number of buckets to fill the `from`-`to` range. When an integer count is given, the actual step is chosen as the finest supported granularity (from 1 millisecond up to 1 day) that produces at most `count` buckets in the `[from, to]` range. Explicit `from` and `to` bounds are required when a count is given.

params.name: from
params.description: Start of the time range that anchors the step grid. Required together with `to`.

params.name: to
params.description: End of the time range. Required together with `from`.

Explicit `from` and `to` bounds are required when a count is given.

examples:
FROM sample_data
| STATS min = MIN(@timestamp), max = MAX(@timestamp) BY bucket = TSTEP(1 hour)
| SORT bucket

FROM sample_data
| STATS min = MIN(@timestamp), max = MAX(@timestamp) BY bucket = TSTEP(1 hour, "2023-10-23T12:15:00.000Z", "2023-10-23T13:55:01.543Z")
| SORT bucket

FROM sample_data
| STATS min = MIN(@timestamp), max = MAX(@timestamp) BY bucket = TSTEP(2, "2023-10-23T12:15:00.000Z", "2023-10-23T13:55:01.543Z")
| SORT bucket
